items.verse: سَيَقُولُونَ ثَلَـٰثَةٌۭ رَّابِعُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ وَيَقُولُونَ خَمْسَةٌۭ سَادِسُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ رَجْمًۢا بِٱلْغَيْبِ ۖ وَيَقُولُونَ سَبْعَةٌۭ وَثَامِنُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ ۚ قُل رَّبِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ بِعِدَّتِهِم مَّا يَعْلَمُهُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌۭ ۗ فَلَا تُمَارِ فِيهِمْ إِلَّا مِرَآءًۭ ظَـٰهِرًۭا وَلَا تَسْتَفْتِ فِيهِم مِّنْهُمْ أَحَدًۭا
items.verseEn: They [i.e., people] will say there were three, the fourth of them being their dog; and they will say there were five, the sixth of them being their dog - guessing at the unseen; and they will say there were seven, and the eighth of them was their dog. Say, [O Muḥammad], "My Lord is most knowing of their number. None knows them except a few. So do not argue about them except with an obvious argument and do not inquire about them among [the speculators] from anyone."

items.verse: لَّوْلَا جَآءُو عَلَيْهِ بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَآءَ ۚ فَإِذْ لَمْ يَأْتُوا۟ بِٱلشُّهَدَآءِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ هُمُ ٱلْكَـٰذِبُونَ
items.verseEn: Why did they [who slandered] not produce for it four witnesses? And when they do not produce the witnesses, then it is they, in the sight of Allāh, who are the liars.

items.verse: قَالَ تَزْرَعُونَ سَبْعَ سِنِينَ دَأَبًۭا فَمَا حَصَدتُّمْ فَذَرُوهُ فِى سُنۢبُلِهِۦٓ إِلَّا قَلِيلًۭا مِّمَّا تَأْكُلُونَ
items.verseEn: [Joseph] said, "You will plant for seven years consecutively; and what you harvest leave in its spikes, except a little from which you will eat.

items.verse: وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ تِسْعَ ءَايَـٰتٍۭ بَيِّنَـٰتٍۢ ۖ فَسْـَٔلْ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ إِذْ جَآءَهُمْ فَقَالَ لَهُۥ فِرْعَوْنُ إِنِّى لَأَظُنُّكَ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ مَسْحُورًۭا
items.verseEn: And We had certainly given Moses nine evident signs, so ask the Children of Israel [about] when he came to them and Pharaoh said to him, "Indeed I think, O Moses, that you are affected by magic."
And for [striking] them are maces of iron.

items.verse: إِنَّآ أَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ سَلَـٰسِلَا۟ وَأَغْلَـٰلًۭا وَسَعِيرًا
items.verseEn: Indeed, We have prepared for the disbelievers chains and shackles and a blaze.
Indeed, with Us [for them] are shackles and burning fire.
And others bound together in irons.

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ وَرَدَتْ مَرَّةً وَاحِدَةً فِي الْقُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Words that appear only once in the Quran

items.verse: قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ سَرْمَدًا إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ مَنْ إِلَـٰهٌ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ يَأْتِيكُم بِضِيَآءٍ ۖ أَفَلَا تَسْمَعُونَ
items.verseEn: Say, "Have you considered: if Allāh should make for you the night continuous until the Day of Resurrection, what deity other than Allāh could bring you light? Then will you not hear?"
That, then, is an unjust division.
Cruel, moreover, and an illegitimate pretender.

items.verse: وَمَا ظَلَمْنَـٰهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۖ فَمَآ أَغْنَتْ عَنْهُمْ ءَالِهَتُهُمُ ٱلَّتِى يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍۢ لَّمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَمَا زَادُوهُمْ غَيْرَ تَتْبِيبٍۢ
items.verseEn: And We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves. And they were not availed at all by their gods which they invoked other than Allāh when there came the command of your Lord. And they did not increase them in other than ruin.

categories.name: أَسْمَاءٌ مُرْتَبِطَةٌ بِأَقْوَامٍ مُهْلَكَةٍ
categories.nameEn: Names associated with destroyed ancient peoples

items.verse: أَهُمْ خَيْرٌ أَمْ قَوْمُ تُبَّعٍۢ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ أَهْلَكْنَـٰهُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ مُجْرِمِينَ
items.verseEn: Are they better or the people of Tubbaʿ and those before them? We destroyed them, [for] indeed, they were criminals.

items.verse: وَعَادًۭا وَثَمُودَا۟ وَأَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلرَّسِّ وَقُرُونًۢا بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ كَثِيرًۭا
items.verseEn: And [We destroyed] ʿAad and Thamūd and the companions of the well and many generations between them.
And the companions of the thicket [i.e., the people of Madyan] were [also] wrongdoers,

harf.hint: The celestial body that marks the day.
harf.arabicVerse: وَٱلشَّمْسُ تَجْرِى لِمُسْتَقَرٍّۢ لَّهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْعَلِيمِ
harf.verse: 36:38 — And the sun runs [on course] toward its stopping point. That is the determination of the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.

deduction.title: The Challenge to a False Deity
deduction.intro: In a land where devotion was misplaced, a lone voice rose to challenge the prevailing falsehood. This messenger of truth confronted a community steeped in idolatry, posing a question that echoed through the ages: would they abandon their hollow worship for the One True Creator? Follow the journey of this steadfast individual who stood against a tide of disbelief to restore the call to monotheism.
clues: I was sent as a messenger to my people, a servant of Allah tasked with guidance.
My community had turned away from the True God, directing their worship to an idol they had named.
I questioned their devotion, asking them, 'Do you call upon this idol and forsake the Best of creators?'
The false god my people worshipped was known by the name Ba'l.
I reminded them that Allah is their Lord and the Lord of their forefathers.
My name is mentioned in the Quran, and peace was sent upon me and those like me ('Ilyasin').

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Hud
Prophet Shu'ayb
Prophet Ilyas (Elijah)
Prophet Musa (Moses)
Prophet Ibrahim

trial.label: What Was My Primary Mission?
options: Building an ark to save believers from a flood
Challenging a tyrant who claimed to give life and death
Splitting the sea to escape a pursuing army
Correcting dishonest practices in weights and measures
Confronting the worship of the idol Ba'l
trial.answer: Confronting the worship of the idol Ba'l

location.label: What Was the Name of the Idol?
options: The Golden Calf
Al-Lat
Wadd
Ba'l
A She-Camel

outcome.label: What Was the Ultimate Record of My Stand?
options: I was remembered as a righteous servant among later generations
My people were destroyed by a mighty flood
I was given a kingdom and the ability to command the jinn
My entire family was saved, except for my wife who lagged behind
I was raised to the heavens by Allah's command
outcome.answer: I was remembered as a righteous servant among later generations

deduction.verse: When he said to his people, "Will you not fear Allāh? (37:124)

translations: Allah has decreed for us He
[so] let the believers put (their) trust [so] let the believers put (their) trust
scramble.hint: A powerful declaration of trust and reliance on Allah's decree (Qadr).
scramble.arabic: قُل لَّن يُصِيبَنَآ إِلَّا مَا كَتَبَ ٱللَّهُ لَنَا هُوَ مَوْلَىٰنَا ۚ وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ
scramble.verseEn: Say, "Never will we be struck except by what Allāh has decreed for us; He is our protector." And upon Allāh let the believers rely.
